regime: Risk-On Rotation (Equities Flip Long, Havens Unwind)
summary: The mirror of Thursday. The post-holiday reopen reversed Jul 2's tech-led de-risk into a clean risk-on rotation. Equities flipped LONG on the 1hr: MNQ +1.34% to 29,946.5 back above a 29,802 stop, and MES +0.98% to 7,596.5 after printing a fresh record 7,602.25. The safe havens that led Jul 2 rolled over: gold spiked to 4,243 overnight then reversed, flipping its 1hr stop SHORT (4,206.1 vs 4,219), and crude flipped short too (68.58 vs 69.03). ISM Services at 54.0, with employment rebounding and prices cooling, gave the tape a soft-landing read. One caution: MNQ closed BELOW its 29,983 session VWAP and faded off the 30,094 high, so the reclaim is real but follow-through is not yet confirmed. Throttle steps up from DEFENSIVE to REDUCED; a TSMOM-20 re-arm is a small starter only, added on a 2-close reclaim of VWAP, not on the first touch.

keyLevels.note: Overhead pivot; a 2-close above confirms follow-through.

keyLevels.note: Session range; faded off the high into the close.

keyLevels.label: MES ATR Stop / record
keyLevels.note: Flipped LONG; fresh all-time high, ES leading.

keyLevels.label: MGC ATR Stop
keyLevels.note: Flipped SHORT after the 4,243 O/N blow-off; price -13 below.

keyLevels.label: MCL ATR Stop
keyLevels.note: Flipped SHORT; crude faded from 69.20, price -0.45 below.

setups.name: TSMOM-20 1hr (re-arm watch)
setups.entry: Small starter only on a 2-close reclaim of the 29,983 VWAP / 30,000 psych
setups.bias: BORDERLINE. Stop flipped long but the below-VWAP close keeps it unconfirmed. Do not pay up at the high.

setups.name: E3v2 Momentum Long 15min
setups.entry: Continuation on a hold above VWAP with ES leading
setups.bias: BORDERLINE. Risk-on regime and an ES record favor momentum; the soft NQ close argues for half size.

setups.name: AVWAP Bounce
setups.entry: Long on a confirmed hold of session AVWAP as support (MNQ 29,983 / MES 7,587)
setups.bias: BORDERLINE. A hold of VWAP is the tell; a clean loss voids it.

event.name: ISM Services PMI (June)
event.actual: 54.0 (vs 54.5 May); 24th straight month of expansion. Employment rebounded to 51.2 (first expansion in 4 months, from 47.9), New Orders 55.1, Prices cooled to 67.7 (first sub-70 since February).
event.surprise: Slightly firm and constructive underneath. After Thursday's soft June jobs (NFP +57K), the market read the pair as a soft-landing package: growth expanding, hiring stabilizing, inflation easing.
event.nqReaction: Reopen reversed Jul 2's de-risk. MNQ gapped up, ran to 30,094, then faded to a 29,946.5 close (+1.34%), stop flipped long.
event.crossAsset: ES led with a fresh record 7,602.25. Gold and crude, the Jul 2 haven winners, rolled over as capital rotated back into equities. Next catalyst: FOMC June minutes Wed Jul 8.

observe.name: GAP_NQ (gap fade)
observe.note: The post-weekend gap up filled higher and held; no clean fade.

observe.name: TSMOM252LO_NQ (long-only 252)
observe.note: The longer-horizon long is intact and reinforced by the short-horizon flip long.

observe.name: ES confirmation
observe.note: MES flipped long with NQ and led to a record, a healthier tape than the Jul 2 both-short read.

risk.throttleState: REDUCED (stepping up from DEFENSIVE). The trend flipped long, but the below-VWAP NQ close plus FOMC minutes Wednesday argue against full size on day one of a reversal.
risk.ddProximity: Not binding. The book entered flat; the full daily budget is intact.
risk.sizing: TSMOM-20 re-arm is a small starter only, added on a 2-close reclaim of 29,983 VWAP / 30,000 psych, not on the first touch. Gold and crude sit just below their stops with tiny gaps, low-conviction fades, not fresh trend shorts.
risk.note: Week ahead: FOMC June minutes Wed Jul 8 is the next catalyst; June CPI mid-July. Keep size modest into the minutes; a hawkish surprise could undo the risk-on flip.

psychology: Do not overpay for the reversal you did not trade. The reclaim happened while the book was flat, that is by design, not a miss. The trap today is FOMO-buying 30,000 after a soft close because you feel behind. Let the level prove itself: a 2-close above the 29,983 VWAP is worth more than a fast fill at the high. Patience after a gap is an edge, not a cost.

COMPASS eligibility (Tue Jul 7): MNQ OR width was wide (161 pts) today; a tighter opening range tomorrow would re-enable the AVWAP Hand-off / Midrange / Back-Into-Range session edges. Hand-off is SHORT-only and needs a failed OR-high break to arm.

Calendar: Tue Jul 7 is light on Tier-1 data; FOMC June minutes land Wed Jul 8 (the next catalyst); June CPI is due mid-July. Conditional, educational framing only.
